title: Placements for Children and Youth - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > Placements for Children and Youth 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter No Filter No Filter Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details ALBERTINA KERR RESIDENTIAL CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides homes for youth with complex needs, supporting social and emotional growth. Residents have private bedrooms and share common areas. Staff help with daily self-care and teach independent living skills, while licensed clinicians provide mental health and behavioral health support. Residents work on goals, coping skills, and self-advocacy in a supportive environment. The program does not offer 2:1 or 1:1 caregiver-to-resident staffing ratios. 424 NE 22nd Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Ages 7-17 with developmental disabilities and mental health challenges Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 239-8101 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JASPER MOUNTAIN JASPER MOUNTAIN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an intensive treatment home with a highly structured environment in a favorable treatment family setting for severely abused and disturbed children. All types of severe emotional and serious behaviors are addressed. However, the program is not designed for children with chronic psychosis or significant developmental disabilities. Proven interventions used are effective with problems such as violent behaviors, sexual acting-out, emotional issues stemming from the effects of childhood trauma, fire-setting, serious difficulties attaching and bonding to others, and delayed social development. 37875 Jasper-Lowell Road Jasper, OR 97438 Eligibility: Ages 3-13 Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Facility: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 747-1235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JASPER MOUNTAIN SAFE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides stabilization, assessment, and family evaluation services to children and their families. The program provides short-term residential treatment for children. Short-term crisis stays are also available for children in need of immediate stabilization (72 hours). 89124 Marcola Road Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: -Ages 3-13 -Signs of serious emotional disturbance -Signs of destructive behavior patterns -In need of immediate stabilization Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Crisis Response Program: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 741-7402 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES STATION 7 STATION 7 SHELTER AND CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth crisis intervention services and a temporary youth shelter. Services include immediate crisis intervention and counseling, youth and family assessments, and referral assistance. Shelter services offer short-term overnight stays, group counseling, and individual case management. Individuals in crisis may also use the shelter for temporary respite while a long-term plan is developed. 931 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Runaway and homeless youth ages 11-20 -Youth and families in conflict Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 689-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides therapeutic support to children, teens, and their families. Services include counseling, therapeutic day treatment schooling programs, and in-home supportive services. Offers therapeutic Foster Care for forster children and teens who have emotional, behavioral, and/or medical needs. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Youth involved in the child welfare or juvenile justice systems Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 436-0333 345 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALBERTINA KERR RESIDENTIAL CARE JASPER MOUNTAIN JASPER MOUNTAIN CENTER JASPER MOUNTAIN SAFE CENTER LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES STATION 7 SHELTER AND CRISIS LINE NEXT DOOR TREATMENT SERVICES
